Site is OpenCart 3.x for Bridge Yachts (Port Dover ON) — sells NEW factory boats (Innovocean/Zodiac/Stanley/Seabreeze inflatables + aluminum) at /boats-for-sale (~96 items across 6 pages of 16) AND used boats at /brokerage-boats/brokerage-used-{sailboats,powerboats} (~48 items). Fix applied: setSettings aiScraping.url=/boats-for-sale, hintScrapingEngineer rewritten with full OpenCart DOM + 3 SRP URLs + pagination + DOM stripping advice + 40-min timeout warning, blockResources extended to font/stylesheet/media. ## ⚠️ DIAGNOSE: Recheck cron (02387484) filters out `active:false` dealers — fix needed (2026-06-18 nightly)
- **Symptom:** Recheck cron consistently reports "ALL entries resolved — no items due" even when `resolved:false` entries with past `recheckAfter` dates exist in recheck-list.json.

- **Example:** Baysville Marina (68d1b8ac8dde55816ed80a16) — recheckAfter 2026-06-18T12:35Z, still unresolved at 10 PM PDT (16h overdue). 6h recheck ran at 05:40 and 11:40 AM, both reported nothing due.
- **Hypothesis:** Filter reads `e.active === true` rather than `!e.resolved && new Date(e.recheckAfter) <= now`. Most inactive/NonPaying dealers have `active: false` in their recheck-list entry.
- **Impact:** ~15 overdue entries silently skipped. Baysville Marina custom v3 unverified.
- **Fix needed:** Update recheck cron payload to filter `!e.resolved && e.recheckAfter && new Date(e.recheckAfter) <= now` (ignore `active` field entirely — `resolved` is the correct gate).
- **Also:** Verify Baysville Marina next non-cron session: `mm scraping:checkScrapingStatus 68d1b8ac8dde55816ed80a16`. Expect ~15u from NonPaying batch. If not yet run, queue Priority run.

- **Dealer that exposed it:** Captain's Choice Marine - North Shore (68d1c7558dde55816ed91370). NonPaying inactive, recheck-list codeFixNeeded +30d.

- **Bug:** `backend/lib/scrapers/MdsBrand/index.ts` `getInventoryPage()` falls back to the HTML load-more path whenever a page's JSON boats are all removed by `locationFilter` (filtered `bikes.length===0`). The HTML path can't read this site's per-boat location → empty location → the `if (this.locationFilter?.length && location)` guard fail-opens → unfiltered (wrong-location) units leak in.

- **Repro:** set Captain's Choice url=`https://www.ccmarine.com/search` + locationFilter=`North Shore` → `mm scraping:testClass mdsBrand '{...}'` returns **125** (29 real North Shore + 96 empty-location main-store leaks) instead of 29.

- **Fix:** track RAW parsed JSON boat count separately from filtered count. Only fall back to HTML when raw JSON boats === 0 (genuinely empty page), NOT when raw>0 but all location-filtered out. Add a guard so HTML path also respects locationFilter (don't fail-open on empty location when a filter is set - or skip HTML entirely when JSON returned data).

- **After merge+deploy:** set Captain's Choice url=`https://www.ccmarine.com/search` + locationFilter=`North Shore`, runScraping, verify **29u** (live North Shore count; old baseline 28u). Settings currently reverted to old ccmnorthshore.com/search (404) as safe failing state.
- **Shared class → PR + evaluator + check no other mdsBrand dealers rely on the HTML-fallback-after-filter behavior.**
